incorrects.description: Student selected 2 groups of 4: miscounted groups and items

params.description: The 3 groups pulse to draw attention.

steps.text: Count the circles first. That tells you the groups. Then count the dots inside one circle. That tells you how many in each group.

incorrects.description: Student selected 4 groups of 2: reversed the group count and item count

params.description: The 3 groups pulse to draw attention.

steps.text: The circles are the groups. Count them: 1, 2, 3. Then look inside one circle and count the dots: 1, 2, 3. So it's 3 groups of 3.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 groups of 4: correct group count but wrong item count

params.description: The dots in the first circle pulse one at a time.

steps.text: You got the groups right: 3 circles means 3 groups. Now count the dots inside one circle carefully. How many dots are in each group?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System counts the 3 circles, then counts the 3 dots in one circle.

steps.text: Let me show you how to see this. First, I count the circles: 1, 2, 3. That's 3 groups. Then I pick one circle and count the dots inside: 1, 2, 3. That's 3 in each group. So the answer is 3 groups of 3. We can write that as 3 × 3.
